company_type: Financial Services - Derivatives Exchange and Clearing

supply_chain.dependencies: Dependence on third-party suppliers for telecommunications networks, data processing, cloud hosting, data center services, and software/hardware. Reliance on ISVs for front-end systems to access trading platforms. Dependence on clearing firms and FCMs for customer access. Critical partnership with Google Cloud for cloud migration and infrastructure.
supply_chain.risks: Interruption or cessation of services by third-party suppliers could negatively impact operations. ISVs may not keep current with platform enhancements. Clearing firm withdrawal could impact trading activity. Google Cloud migration involves execution risk and potential system failures during transition. Concentration risk with data center provider CyrusOne.

regulatory_risks: Implementation of transaction tax or user fee in U.S., U.K., E.U., Illinois, or New Jersey
Elimination of 60/40 tax treatment for futures and options contracts
EU Benchmark Regulation provisions and transitional period extensions
Prohibition or restriction of exclusive licenses for benchmark indices in Europe
Negative treatment of U.S. Treasury securities liquidity profile or collateral limitations
New regulations governing cryptocurrency markets
New treasury market structure regulations including clearing obligations
SEC rule mandating central clearing of U.S. Treasury securities and repurchase agreements (effective December 2026 and June 2027)
Basel III Endgame rules and capital framework changes for banks
Financial Stability Board (FSB) proposal for CCPs to maintain separate resolution resources
Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) requirements for EU financial entities
CFTC regulatory requirements for derivatives clearing organizations and designated contract markets
European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) regulations
UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) regulations
Netherlands Authority for the Financial Markets (AFM) regulations
German Federal Financial Supervisory Authority (BaFin) regulations
FINRA and SEC regulations for broker-dealer operations
MiFID II requirements
EMIR and EMIR 3.0 regulations
Market Abuse Regulation
Capital Requirements Directive IV
Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act compliance
Designation as systemically important financial market utility with additional Federal Reserve oversight
Benchmark administration regulations under UK Benchmark Regulations

sanction_risks: Impact of tariffs and tax policy changes
Restrictions on ability to offer products in specific geographies
Limitations on underlying/physical product flows across geographies due to sanctions
Geopolitical conflicts in Middle East and Eastern Europe affecting energy markets
